expectations: Phase 1 dispatches a Reproduction or Dependency/Environment agent in addition to Error and Change Analysis
Investigation identifies the fixed sleep(2000) as a timing assumption that doesn't account for variable fulfillment time (500ms-3000ms)
Investigation correlates the failure onset with the CI runner downgrade from 4-core to 2-core machines 2 weeks ago
Synthesis cross-references the timing: fulfillment takes up to 3000ms but test only waits 2000ms, and slower CI runners make this worse
Phase 3 hypothesis identifies the fragile sleep-based waiting pattern as the root cause, not a code bug in the order processing logic
Phase 3.5 red-team verifies the hypothesis explains the 30% failure rate (correlates with fulfillment time distribution exceeding 2s more often on slower hardware)
Phase 4 fix replaces the fixed sleep with condition-based polling or retry-until-condition (e.g., poll order status with timeout)
Phase 4 implementer writes a test that proves the sleep-based approach fails under slow conditions before implementing the fix
Phase 5 quality gate is run on the fix to verify it doesn't introduce flaky behavior of its own
Orchestrator identifies this as a test infrastructure issue, not an application code bug, and communicates this distinction clearly
